title: rupertia physodes
intro: Rupertia physodes, also known as Shrubby Lupine, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Rupertia physodes can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional Maori remedies, such as poultices and infusions, to support overall well-being.
